name: Qatar
experiences: Museum of Islamic Art waterfront galleries
National Museum of Qatar desert-rose architecture
Souq Waqif evenings and falcon souq lanes
Katara Cultural Village and beach promenade
avoid: Long outdoor afternoons from May to September because heat and humidity rise fast
Depending only on the metro once you leave Doha and Al Wakrah
Driving to Zekreet late without fuel, shade, and offline maps
stopoverNote: Qatar works especially well as a 4- to 6-day premium stopover from India, with Doha as the main base and easy day trips north and west.

cities.name: Doha
cities.notes: Museum-heavy waterfront capital with Souq Waqif, Katara, The Pearl, Doha Metro and the country's easiest hotel base

cities.name: Al Wakrah
cities.notes: Relaxed southern coastal town with a restored souq, dhow-lined seafront and direct Doha Metro access

cities.name: Al Khor
cities.notes: Northern coast base for Purple Island, Al Thakira mangroves, the corniche and easy resort stays

cities.name: Zekreet
cities.notes: West-coast desert outpost for Richard Serra's sculpture, Film City, limestone formations and Ras Abrouq sunsets

itinerary.sim: Ooredoo or Vodafone Qatar visitor SIMs usually land around ₹675–₹1,800 depending on data, enough for Doha Metro, Careem, Google Maps, restaurant bookings and a Zekreet day trip.

Doha.name: Doha
Doha.note: Use Doha as the core base because metro coverage, museum density and the biggest hotel range make the rest of Qatar much easier.

days.theme: National Museum storytelling, Corniche air, and a first-night Souq Waqif reset

activities.name: Souq Waqif and Falcon Souq lanes

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Doha Old Town

days.theme: Museum of Islamic Art, Katara, and The Pearl on a culture-to-marina day

activities.name: MIA Park and dhow harbour walk

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Doha Old Town

days.theme: Easy metro day, skyline stops, and flexible café time before the road-based excursions begin

activities.name: Mina District and Box Park waterfront stroll
Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab Mosque exterior and visit
West Bay or Lusail evening taxi loop

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Doha Old Town

days.theme: Use the extra Doha day for galleries, beach clubs, or a slower luxury hotel stay

Al Wakrah.name: Al Wakrah
Al Wakrah.note: Most travelers do Al Wakrah as a metro-and-taxi day trip from Doha, though the souq hotel area works well for a quieter overnight.

days.theme: Beachfront souq wandering and an easy coastal change of pace south of Doha

activities.name: Doha Metro Red Line rides and short local taxi hops
Al Janoub Stadium exterior and park

hotels.name: Souq Al Wakra Hotel Qatar by Tivoli

Al Khor.name: Al Khor
Al Khor.note: A rental car makes Al Khor easiest because Purple Island and Al Thakira are awkward without your own wheels.

days.theme: Mangroves, Purple Island, and a resort-backed northern coast day

activities.name: Rental car for the Doha or Al Wakrah to Al Khor round trip
Purple Island boardwalk and mangrove trail
Al Khor Corniche and Al Bayt Park evening stop

Zekreet.name: Zekreet
Zekreet.note: Go early, carry water, and do not count on public transport; this is the part of Qatar where a rental car or guided 4x4 is essential.

days.theme: West-coast sculpture, film-set ruins, and desert-sea light near Ras Abrouq

activities.name: Rental car or guided 4x4 day trip from Doha
Ras Abrouq beach and limestone formation stops

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Doha Old Town

connections.method: Doha Metro Red Line to Al Wakrah with a short taxi for the beach and souq stretch
Self-drive or taxi across Doha's orbital roads; metro does not cover this northbound jump
Drive back through Doha toward Ras Abrouq; there is no practical public transport option

extras: This route works best as a 6-day Qatar circuit with Doha as the main base, Al Wakrah as a light metro day, Al Khor as a north-coast outing, and Zekreet as the one road-trip-heavy day.
Museum of Islamic Art and the National Museum of Qatar currently price standard non-resident entry at QAR 50 each, which converts to about ₹1,125 using the requested QAR-to-INR rate.
Souq Waqif, The Pearl-Qatar, and Katara Cultural Village are all easy free-entry anchors, so the trip's spend usually comes from hotels, taxis, and optional kayaking or west-coast tours.

Doha Metro is the cheapest way to move around the capital at roughly ₹45 per ride or ₹135 for a day pass, but taxis and ride-hailing become the practical backup once you leave the central museum-and-souq corridor.

For Al Khor and especially Zekreet, a rental car is usually better value than chaining long taxis because you control timing around Purple Island tides, Serra sculpture light, and the remote Ras Abrouq stretch.
